entries.promptText: Instrumental background loop for an early-round festival routing game. Mood: morning setup energy, relaxed confidence, airy and optimistic. Style: indie-electronic with soft kick, brushed percussion, warm bass, subtle guitar or plucky synth motifs. Tempo: 96-104 BPM. Structure: seamless loop, stable groove, no long intro/outro, no sudden breakdowns. Constraints: no vocals, no spoken words, no harsh transients, clean mobile-friendly low-end.

Instrumental background loop for mid-round festival gameplay. Mood: forward momentum, crowd-building urgency, bright and focused. Style: dance-pop/electro groove with tight drums, syncopated hats, punchy bass, short synth hooks. Tempo: 118-124 BPM. Structure: seamless loop with consistent pulse, minimal arrangement drift. Constraints: no vocals, no spoken words, avoid brittle highs, keep hooks short and repeatable.

Instrumental background loop for late-round peak festival pressure. Mood: intense, triumphant, headliner-night adrenaline. Style: electro-rock crossover with strong kick-snare backbone, sidechained synth layers, festival-scale riser texture. Tempo: 128-134 BPM. Structure: loop-ready with clear downbeat continuity and no hard cutoff tail. Constraints: no vocals, no spoken words, powerful but controlled low-end, avoid distortion fatigue.

Short gameplay SFX for artist spawn. Tone: bright welcoming electronic ping with a tiny crowd-air tail. Envelope: fast attack, short decay. Duration: about 0.8-1.0 seconds. Constraints: clean transient, no clipping, readable over music.

Touch interaction SFX for drawing artist path lines. Tone: smooth airy swipe with light friction texture. Envelope: soft attack, short trail, no click at end. Duration: about 0.9-1.2 seconds. Constraints: subtle and non-intrusive, no harsh highs.

Positive delivery confirmation SFX for reaching a stage. Tone: concise celebratory hit with small crowd-lift character. Envelope: fast attack, medium-short decay. Duration: about 1.1-1.4 seconds. Constraints: uplifting but not oversized, no clipping.

Hero combo-delivery SFX for chained successful sets. Tone: bigger celebratory impact than standard delivery, sparkling upper flourish, energetic crowd accent. Envelope: fast attack, controlled tail. Duration: about 1.6-2.0 seconds. Constraints: high excitement, still clean and mobile-safe.

Gameplay miss feedback SFX when artist route fails. Tone: gentle descending motif with subtle disappointment texture. Envelope: moderate attack, short-mid decay. Duration: about 1.2-1.6 seconds. Constraints: informative not punishing, avoid harsh buzzer style.

Short SFX for two artists stopping to chat. Tone: playful social interruption cue with warm non-verbal texture. Envelope: quick and light. Duration: about 0.7-1.0 seconds. Constraints: non-annoying, no intelligible words.

SFX for distraction encounter trigger (merch, food, fan pull). Tone: curious attention-grab with slight whimsical sparkle. Envelope: quick rise, short decay. Duration: about 1.0-1.3 seconds. Constraints: must be distinct from miss cue and timer warning.

Round completion fanfare SFX for festival game. Tone: triumphant compact stinger, uplifting and polished. Envelope: strong opening, quick celebratory release. Duration: about 2.2-2.6 seconds. Constraints: cinematic but concise, no muddy low-end.

Round failed stinger SFX for festival game. Tone: empathetic downward cadence, emotional but quick reset energy. Envelope: soft impact then short resolve. Duration: about 1.8-2.2 seconds. Constraints: avoid harsh alarm feel, keep it tasteful.

Urgency SFX for low-time warning during gameplay. Tone: rhythmic alert pulse with clear high-mid definition. Envelope: fast, percussive, repeat-safe. Duration: about 0.8-1.0 seconds. Constraints: loop-friendly cadence, no piercing frequencies, must cut through mix.
